name.fr: Semita de piña (pâtisserie levée salvadorienne à l'ananas)

description.en: A yeasted bread dough sandwiching pineapple jam, baked flat in a big pan and cut into squares rather than shaped as a loaf. A Salvadoran bakery favorite, its lattice-topped surface signals the jam hidden inside.
description.de: Ein hefegetriebener Brotteig mit einer Ananasmarmelade-Füllung, flach in einer großen Form gebacken und in Quadrate geschnitten statt zu einem Brot geformt. Ein Bäckereiliebling aus El Salvador, dessen Gitteroberfläche die versteckte Marmelade verrät.
description.es: Una masa de pan con levadura que envuelve mermelada de piña, horneada plana en un molde grande y cortada en cuadros en vez de darle forma de hogaza. Un favorito de las panaderías salvadoreñas, con su superficie de rejilla que delata la mermelada escondida.
description.fr: Une pâte à pain levée enveloppant une confiture d'ananas, cuite à plat dans un grand moule et découpée en carrés plutôt que façonnée en pain. Une favorite des boulangeries salvadoriennes, à la surface en treillis qui trahit la confiture cachée à l'intérieur.

origin.region: El Salvador (also Guatemala and Honduras, with local variants)
origin.note: A yeasted bread dough, not a pastry crust, sandwiches the jam filling and bakes flat in a large pan before being cut into squares; pineapple is the most traditional filling, though mango or guava versions exist.

yield.shape: large flat rectangular tart cut into 12 squares

keeping.note: Keeps well for several days at room temperature under a cloth; the jam filling keeps the crumb moist longer than a plain sweet roll.

method.title: Warm the enrichments
method.summary: Heat milk, butter, sugar and salt together until the butter melts, then cool to lukewarm.

method.title: Bloom yeast
method.summary: Dissolve the yeast in warm water and let stand until foamy.

method.title: Mix & knead
method.summary: Combine the milk mixture, yeast mixture, beaten eggs and flour into a dough, then knead until smooth and elastic.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Cover and let rise until doubled, about 1 to 1.5 hours.

method.title: Layer & fill
method.summary: Press half the dough into a greased large baking pan, spread with the cinnamon-pineapple filling leaving a border, then top with a second sheet of dough and seal the edges.

method.title: Decorate & rest
method.summary: Lay a lattice of dough strips over the top, brush with egg wash, sprinkle with sugar, and rest 30 minutes.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Prick the surface all over, then bake until golden brown, 30-40 minutes; cool before cutting into squares.

source.adaptation: formula.* covers the base dough only; the pineapple filling, lattice trim and egg-sugar topping are excluded from the percentages. Cup measures converted to grams via standard baker's conversions.
